name: Ahmed Abdul-Malik
abstract: Ahmed Abdul-Malik (born Jonathan Tim Jr.; January 30, 1927 – October 2, 1993) was an American jazz double bassist and oud player. Abdul-Malik is remembered for integrating Middle Eastern and North African music styles in his jazz music.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I had Ahmed Abdul- Malik on the oud; I had William Allen on bass, myself at the piano... We toured, the first time, to play Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I was on some panel at Princeton University with a cast that included Ralph de Toledano, Ahmed Abdul Malik, and Benny Carter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Ahmed Abdul-Malik? [INTERVIEWEE] …the guy had a beautiful sound; he was a wonderful player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
